rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical hydroxy-thiocarbamation of electron-deficient alkenes (α-arylacrylates) with KSCN and H2O to give β-hydroxy primary S-thiocarbamates
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of SCN- to thiocyanate radical (SCN•) which adds to the alkene; cathode reduces H2O/protons to evolve H2 (confirmed by Pd/C-catalyzed hydrogenation of N-phenylmaleimide using the evolved H2)

key_intermediates.name: benzylic C-radical from SCN• addition to α-phenylacrylate
benzylic cation after further anodic oxidation
β-hydroxy primary S-thiocarbamate product 4a (after SCN hydrolysis)

electron_flow: SCN- is oxidized at the anode to SCN•, which adds to the β-carbon of the α-arylacrylate to give a stabilized benzylic radical; further anodic single-electron oxidation generates a benzylic cation that is trapped by H2O to install the OH, while the cathode reduces protons to H2. The pendant SCN is then hydrolyzed to the primary S-thiocarbamate.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 4a formed; BHT-SCN adduct (HRMS m/z 300.1400, calc 300.1393) and BHT-trapped benzylic radical adduct (HRMS m/z 462.2076, calc 462.2073) detected, supporting SCN• and a C-centered radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: D2O
probe_reagents.observation: D-incorporation into OH and NH2 of 4a (D-4a, HRMS 281.0643 vs calc 281.0646), confirming H2O is the proton/H source for OH and NH

probe_reagents.name: H2-18O
probe_reagents.observation: 18O incorporated into both the OH and the C=O of the thiocarbamate of 4a (18O-4a, HRMS 282.0549 vs calc 282.0542), confirming H2O is the oxygen source for hydroxyl and for carbamate carbonyl (SCN hydrolysis)

probe_reagents.name: α-phenylacrylate 1a
probe_reagents.observation: CV onset oxidation +1.693 V vs SCE

probe_reagents.name: KSCN
probe_reagents.observation: CV onset oxidation +0.383 V vs SCE — much easier to oxidize than 1a, indicating SCN- is the species oxidized at the anode

probe_reagents.name: N-phenylmaleimide (H2 probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Headspace H2 from electrolysis reduces N-phenylmaleimide to N-phenylsuccinimide 6a in 59% yield with Pd/C, confirming cathodic H2 evolution

notes: Authors depict two BHT adducts (I: BHT-SCN from direct trapping of SCN•; II: BHT-trapped benzylic radical after SCN• addition to alkene), supporting a stepwise radical addition / radical-polar crossover pathway. The primary S-thiocarbamate (-SC(=O)NH2) arises from hydrolysis of the intermediate thiocyanate (-SCN) by H2O, as evidenced by 18O incorporation into the carbamate carbonyl.
